response.justification: The role requires 8 years of C++ programming and 5 years of large-scale distributed systems/infrastructure experience, but the candidate's C++ exposure is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015. Their core stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang with web, mobile, and pricing-platform work — no demonstrated cloud compute platform, distributed systems at scale, or formal technical leadership of cloud/DevOps engineers. Strong craftsmanship and breadth, but a poor match for a C++-centric cloud compute Tech Lead role.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development)
Demonstrated mentoring and teaching via workshops, dojos, and Selenium course
Broad polyglot experience across backend, frontend, embedded, and tooling
concerns: Only ~6 months of C++ experience versus the 8-year minimum requirement
No evidence of large-scale cloud compute platform or distributed systems leadership
No formal technical leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers

key_factors.factor: C++ experience gap (8 yrs required, ~0.5 yr shown)
Lack of large-scale cloud compute / distributed systems work
Solid mentoring and craftsmanship track record
